title: Spaghetti alla Carbonara Classic

seo.title: Spaghetti Carbonara: Authentic Classic Italian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Italian Spaghetti Carbonara! Learn to make this creamy, rich pasta dish with authentic ingredients like guanciale and Pecorino Romano.
seo.h1: Classic Spaghetti Carbonara: The Authentic Italian Recipe
h2Headings: Pro Tips for Perfect Spaghetti Carbonara
seo.imageAlt: A plate of classic spaghetti carbonara with crispy guanciale, creamy egg sauce, and fresh black pepper.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our classic Spaghetti Carbonara, a true taste of Italy.

description: Spaghetti alla Carbonara is a timeless Roman pasta dish renowned for its rich, creamy texture and savory depth. This authentic recipe shuns cream, instead creating a luscious sauce from egg yolks, Pecorino Romano cheese, cured guanciale, and plenty of black pepper. It is a simple yet profoundly satisfying meal that celebrates the essence of Italian culinary tradition.

ingredients.notes: or other long pasta like bucatini
cured pork jowl, cut into small cubes or strips
finely grated, plus extra for serving
freshly ground, plus extra for serving

instructions.title: Prepare the Egg and Cheese Mixture
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, whisk together the egg yolks and the whole egg. Add the grated Pecorino Romano cheese and a generous amount of freshly ground black pepper. Mix well until a thick, creamy paste forms. Set aside.

instructions.title: Cook the Guanciale
instructions.text: Place the diced guanciale in a large cold skillet (preferably non-stick) over medium-low heat. Cook slowly, stirring occasionally, until the guanciale is crispy and has rendered its fat. This should take about 8-10 minutes. Remove the crispy guanciale with a slotted spoon and set aside, leaving the rendered fat in the skillet.

instructions.title: Boil the Spaghetti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a rolling boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package directions until al dente. Before draining, reserve about 1 to 1.5 cups of the starchy pasta water.

instructions.title: Combine Pasta and Guanciale Fat
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the drained spaghetti directly into the skillet with the warm guanciale fat. Toss the spaghetti thoroughly to coat it evenly with the flavorful fat. This helps prevent the pasta from sticking and primes it for the sauce.

instructions.title: Create the Carbonara Sauce
instructions.text: Remove the skillet from the heat. Wait about 30 seconds for the pasta to cool slightly. Gradually add the egg and cheese mixture to the spaghetti, continuously tossing vigorously with tongs. Slowly add a ladleful (about 1/4 cup) of the reserved hot pasta water while continuing to toss. The heat from the pasta and the steam from the water will cook the eggs gently, creating a creamy, emulsified sauce without scrambling. Add more pasta water, a tablespoon at a time, if needed to achieve your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in most of the crispy guanciale, reserving a small amount for garnish. Serve the Spaghetti alla Carbonara immediately in warm bowls. Garnish with the remaining crispy guanciale, extra grated Pecorino Romano cheese, and a final generous grinding of fresh black pepper.

tips: The key to a creamy Carbonara is temperature control. Ensure the pasta is hot but not scorching when adding the egg mixture to prevent scrambling. Removing the pan from direct heat is crucial.
Always use guanciale for authentic Carbonara. Its unique fat and flavor are irreplaceable. If unavailable, high-quality pancetta is a distant second-best, but never use bacon.
Reserve plenty of pasta water. It is essential for emulsifying the sauce and achieving the perfect creamy consistency. Add it gradually until the sauce coats the pasta beautifully.
article: Spaghetti alla Carbonara Classic is a beloved Italian Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
